active_conjecture.summary: The active conjecture is no longer about the fixed stable family. It asks whether bounded-support commutant observables can be synthesized uniformly from arbitrary sampled partition labels, retain inverse-polynomial normalized gaps across broad Kronecker support, and feed a branch-weighted frame whose outcomes decode the hidden involution. Finite typical profiles are only stress tests.

execution_model: Research generation and experiments run interactively during active Codex sessions. A GitHub workflow validates pushed snapshots, and a scheduled backup can commit only validation-passing changes; neither mechanism performs autonomous quantum-algorithm research between sessions.

milestones.detail: The proof gate requires reductions, classical baselines, falsifiers, and an explicit path from the input model to every conditioned state or sector.
milestones.title: Candidate admission now prices natural access

milestones.detail: Shape, first-stage, and second-stage observables give all 25 encoded labels on both trees and a polynomial left/right relabelling isometry.
milestones.title: The stable coupling-tree interface is complete

milestones.detail: Fifty-four exact character-ratio inequalities prove lambda_min(F)>=(71/825)n^-5 and polynomial inverse-square-root filters for two involution families.
milestones.title: The stable three-copy frame is filterable

milestones.detail: Its exact probability is d_W^3 d_xi Tr(F)/(n!)^3, at most (25/3)n^9/(n!)^3; postselection and generic amplification are superpolynomial.
milestones.title: Natural access kills the fixed stable branch

milestones.detail: For fixed K, total weak-Fourier mass is at most 2 P_K n^(2K)/n!. Uniform adaptation to typical high-dimensional partitions is now mandatory.
milestones.title: Every fixed bounded-tail route is cut

next_actions.detail: Define orbit-sum block encodings uniformly from arbitrary partition descriptions and seek character-only normalized-gap certificates.
next_actions.title: Build typical-label commutant observables

next_actions.detail: Aggregate over naturally sampled labels instead of postselecting sectors, and prove the retained mass remains inverse polynomial.
next_actions.title: Keep natural branch mass in every theorem

next_actions.detail: Specify the hidden-involution-dependent measurement distribution, construct a decoder, and try to reproduce it with classical character and tensor methods.
next_actions.title: Derive and attack an outcome law

overview: The project is a proof and falsification engine, not an algorithm demo. Its strongest constructive result is a polynomially filterable three-copy stable frame. Its strongest negative result is more important: every predetermined bounded-tail Fourier family has factorially small natural mass. The nonabelian route must now work uniformly on naturally sampled high-dimensional partitions.

tracks.evidence: The all-target census evaluated millions of legal targets; the remaining question is a source-conditioned reduced-basis theorem, not another target sweep.
tracks.next: Prove a random-label LLL geometry law or abandon nearest-plane branching.
tracks.summary: Exact source models, sieve accounting, lattice attacks, and marker-aware decoders were implemented. Fixed-depth nearest-plane lists eventually collapse, so small-n decoder success is not evidence of a polynomial algorithm.
tracks.title: Hidden shift / DHSP

tracks.evidence: Current triage retains 0 viable code rows; the last Goppa syzygy collision was resolved by the hull-projector route.
tracks.next: Generate a new natural family that survives the complete classical triage gate.
tracks.summary: Goppa, quasi-cyclic, finite-geometry, rank-metric, and CFI-derived rows were attacked with support, hull, Schur, syzygy, projector, recovery, and graph-side invariants.
tracks.title: Code equivalence

tracks.evidence: Encoded labels: 25/25. All-n conditioning families: 2. Naturally accessible fixed branches: 0. At n=20 the typical-source audit reaches multiplicity 6,408,361.
tracks.next: Transfer bounded-support observables to naturally sampled typical partitions with uniform gaps and no postselection.
tracks.status: Stable route cut; typical-label frontier active
tracks.summary: One fixed stable family now has complete encoded coupling-tree labels, a direct three-copy frame block encoding, an all-n eigenvalue lower bound, and polynomial inverse filters. Exact access accounting proves that branch, and every fixed bounded-tail extension, is naturally inaccessible.
tracks.title: Nonabelian coset states

verdict.detail: Every speedup claim remains blocked. A complete 25-label stable coupling-tree interface, direct frame block encoding, all-n conditioning bound, and polynomial inverse filter are proved. The same audit then cuts this branch as an algorithmic route: its natural-input probability is factorially small.
verdict.title: No breakthrough yet
